source.title: Border defense setup guide (Littleton) — merged from two uploads
source.notes: Two transcripts from the same creator (Littleton) covering the same four sites (Top Floor Armory/Archives/Office, Bathroom/Tellers, Ventilation/Workshop, Customs/Supply) were merged into this single file. v2 was treated as primary where the two disagree, with v1 used to fill in detail v2 skips. Both are auto-generated YouTube captions — expect ASR errors, especially on operator names and jargon. Room names are the creator's own informal callouts, not verified wiki callouts. No reliable top-down blueprint grounding was available for this map during the rewrite pass, so location strings are room/feature names only — no compass directions have been added; do not assume north-is-up accuracy for this file the way bank.json has it.

reinforcements.location: Two main Armory Walls
reinforcements.note: Reinforcing both is the safer default. Can instead reinforce just the left side and leave the right side soft to play a deployable shield aggressively onto the walkway — bring a DMR, repel angles stack with the wall angles here.

reinforcements.location: Back-Right Wall and Rear Wall of Archives
reinforcements.note: Both reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Single Wall in Office
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Triple Wall in Office
reinforcements.note: Reinforce by default. At higher ELO, leave feet holes instead so an impact grenade can be thrown through to destroy an ACE charge placed on the wall.

reinforcements.location: Right-Hand Walls near Break Room (two walls)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — lets you hold toward Break Room.

reinforcements.location: Wall near the Balcony / Rotate into Fountain-Brazil
reinforcements.note: Judgment call — leaving it soft gives an angle onto the balcony and a second position to hold the door from. Reinforcing denies an East Side push (Gridlock/Ying/Monty into Archives) an extra route.

headHoles.location: Wall Between Half Wall Room and Fountain/Office Door
headHoles.note: Do not reinforce this wall. Leave it soft and make feet holes instead — reinforcing it lets attackers plant right on the door with no challenge angle except a slow flank.

Keep holes low, at the base of the wall, so C4 can be thrown over the top (a smoke thrown the same way hits the ceiling first). Watches the east plant/rush. Don't bring the line down too far or it gives attackers an angle back at you.

headHoles.location: Hole Watching the Vents Window Jump-In
headHoles.note: Made toward the start of the round to catch an early jumper.

headHoles.location: Barricade at the Front Door, Second Panel
headHoles.note: Shoot out the second panel, not the first. Attackers commonly spawn-peek and shoot the default cam while the barricade still looks closed — this hole lets you spawn-peek them back.

headHoles.location: Wall/Carpet Angle on the Office Window (Talis Side)
headHoles.note: Watches anyone hopping into the office window. The carpet decoration takes an extra shot to clear.

headHoles.location: Wall near the Talis-Side Window, Top-Right Corner
headHoles.note: Line-of-sight hole, not a full reinforce. Catches an attacker jumping the window unspotted early round.

rotates.location: Rotate from Half Wall/Behind-Bomb to the Door
rotates.note: Use if attackers plant Armory and hold behind the bomb — approach crouched, using it as cover, instead of walking straight through the door.

Watches CC window, CC door, holds Break Room door and Break Balcony, and lets you reposition unpredictably. Brief the team before using it — don't cut it solo unless everyone knows it exists. Always go prone before opening this hole, not standing — standing risks shooting down through the wall and opening the hatch below into Small Office.
rotates.timestamp: 05:34 / v1; also v2 05:58; safety note 07:18 / v1

rotates.location: Rotate near the Triple-Wall Side, toward Top East
rotates.note: Lets you aggress onto Top East or fight onto the Break door and return.

hatches.location: Office Hatch (drops to Small Office below)
hatches.note: Discrepancy between sources: v1 leaves it open with one shotgun hole to catch early Talis window-jumpers — free pick plus an escape route if pushed. v2 reinforces it as an anchor point for the player below. Decide based on whether you have a player committed to holding below.

gadgetPlacements.location: Behind the Half Wall Door, near the Door
gadgetPlacements.note: Covers the door and can't be shot from the door itself. Also holds the window from this position.
Goo mines on the window, the door, and the Break Balcony window/door for an audio alert. Both carry shotguns. Still be careful opening the rotate.
Mutes off the double door while watching the office window.

operatorRecommendations.note: Jammer near the Half Wall door covers the plant and can't be shot back at from the door; also holds the desks room watching the double door and office window.
Goo mines around the rotate give early warning. Both carry shotguns for close fights in that room.
For playing the soft right-hand side of Armory aggressively with a shield — DMR needed for tight angles against repel spots.

generalTips.note: Both the east push (Archives) and west push (Armory) are considered equally valid by attackers.
Border's wall texture makes punch holes less obvious — a crouched punch hole into Armory from Archives is a sneaky option.
Vertical C4 lineup: the floor below the door plant spot is soft — a C4 dropped from above lands there.
Soft-floor vert spot behind the Archives plant area drops into Bathroom below — watch for C4 dropped on the wrong spot (a prox mine, not the plant) by mistake.
The office window (drop-in point to Talis below) can be watched/shotgunned from below.
The 90 cam can be shot from the wall near the rotate if it isn't reinforced — know attackers can deny your cam from here.
Half Wall is a stronger anchor position since the under-hatch grenade tech (cooking a nade from the vents window below) was patched out.
Keep at least one player roaming underneath (Bathroom/Workshop side) in addition to the top floor hold.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls next to the Tellers Windows
reinforcements.note: Reinforced. Do not reinforce between the sides.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls from Bathroom into Back of Workshop
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — extends the effective site boundary from Bathroom out into the back of Workshop rather than treating Bathroom as the last line.

reinforcements.location: Four Walls at the Back/Bottom of Workshop
reinforcements.note: Reinforced. Uses most of the site's allotment.

reinforcements.location: Wall from Bathroom toward Outside
reinforcements.note: Judgment call — reinforcing denies attackers a long line of sight through Bathroom toward Workshop; leaving it soft keeps flexibility to hold the Tellers window and line up a raised C4 hole onto the default plant spot.

reinforcements.location: Window-Side Wall above Small Office
reinforcements.note: Catches anyone hopping into the office window from below. The carpet decoration takes an extra shot to clear.

headHoles.note: Feet holes for the default plant — visible even from a retake angle.

headHoles.location: Head Holes on the Talis Side Window Jump-In
Drone Hole near the Hatch Drop Spot
headHoles.note: Set up at the start of the round. Shows attacker ankles if they drop the hatch and start planting. Reposition it if needed.

rotates.location: Rotate between the Two Sides of Bathroom, near the Stool
rotates.note: Use if caught on the wrong side when a window gets opened and shot through — lets you challenge from a better angle instead of exposing wide.
Head holes here also see the side window and Talis window jump-ins.
Cut the hole on the correct side of the wall — the wrong side gives attackers a line of sight through the whole site.

rotates.location: Rotate to Avoid the Exposed Window between Bathroom Sub-Areas
rotates.note: Lets you tuck up and use cover instead of crossing the exposed area.

hatches.note: Default to leaving it open with two players roaming upstairs who can drop late — attackers usually bring hard breach anyway. If nobody plays upstairs, reinforce it and pull a reinforcement off a back Workshop wall instead.

gadgetPlacements.note: Holds the gap where someone jumps the vents window — needs an ACOG, not reliable with an SMG.
Trap or intel operator upstairs gives info on the approach into this site from above.

operatorRecommendations.note: ACOG needed to hold the vents-window gap reliably from the back corner.
Covers the site from above — near-mandatory since this site depends heavily on holding upstairs.

generalTips.note: Most of this setup exists outside the site itself to support a late retake — spending prolonged time directly in Bathroom/Tellers is not the plan.
Rated as probably the second-best site on the map, arguably stronger than the Top Floor Armory site. Holding Workshop and having someone roaming above are both key.
One of the easier sites to attack overall if not defended carefully away from the site itself.
Need an ACOG watching one direction and someone else watching the other — most time is spent around Workshop.

reinforcements.location: One Wall in Vents (Window-Spray Wall)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — attackers commonly stand outside the window and spray through this wall. Leave the other Vents wall soft with feet holes watching anyone walking in.

reinforcements.location: Server Hatch Area Walls, Back of Workshop (four walls)
reinforcements.note: All four reinforced. Bring a prox mine on this side too.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls into Bathroom (Back)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — extends Workshop's boundary into Bathroom rather than stopping at Workshop's own wall.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls into Blue
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Wall from Supply Side
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — if soft, someone in Supply can cut a sightline all the way back through Workshop.

reinforcements.location: Two Armory Walls and Back Office Wall (Upstairs)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced upstairs — stops attackers spraying through the office-side window and splitting the top floor.

reinforcements.location: Top Wall on the Main Armory Breach (Upstairs)
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Second Wall from the Right of Workshop
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Jail Walls (Optional)
reinforcements.note: Optional use of reinforcements to cover Jail instead — easy no-door entry point for a hard breach. Recommended at higher ELO.

headHoles.location: Line of Sight onto the Vents Window
Line of Sight Covering the Vents Door
headHoles.note: Holds anyone entering the door and the window from the same area.

headHoles.location: Line of Sight to the Back Corner Plant Spot
headHoles.note: Covers the back-corner plant spot attackers retreat to after taking the window.
Also a generic line of sight onto a common late-round plant spot at the back corner of Workshop.

rotates.location: Rotate in the Corner near Vents, plus a Hole Further Right
Rotate on the Far-Right Wall (Upstairs Side)
rotates.note: Cut as tight to the wall as possible. Holds the front door and Vents window from a different angle.

rotates.location: Rotate at the Very Back of Bathroom
rotates.note: Holds a line of sight through the gap if someone jumps the Vents window (ACOG-dependent). Also lets you push into Blue or toward the bottom of Metal Stairs, and is the only way out if trapped in the back of Workshop.

hatches.note: Always leave open — attackers get very little from it and it forces them into a choke point you can hold. Both sources agree.
Default to open if roamers are upstairs. Reinforce it if nobody's playing upstairs, and pull a reinforcement off a back Workshop/Bathroom wall instead.

gadgetPlacements.note: Covers the gap if someone jumps the Vents window; also watches the common late-round plant spot in this corner.
Same upstairs-hold requirement as other sites.

operatorRecommendations.note: Watches the Vents/Workshop direction with an ACOG-capable loadout.
This site relies heavily on defending the Armory/Office area above it, same as Bathroom/Tellers.

generalTips.note: This site's effective boundary is drawn down the hallway — the far side toward Bathroom/Tellers doesn't need active defending from this site's players.
If the wall near the back of the bomb and the hatch are both soft/open, attackers can get a strong angle from behind — watch for this as a defender.
Common plant spots to watch: behind a specific table (two sides of it), and the back corner of Workshop.
Rated worse than Bathroom/Tellers but better than Customs/Supply.
Used to be considered the best site on the map before the rework, now second to Bathroom. Key is holding above and holding upstairs.

reinforcements.location: Two Jail Walls
reinforcements.note: Reinforced. This area is soft above, so keeping it fully closed all round is tough — attackers can open the ceiling and nade over the top. Watch for repel players (Blue Book/Sledge) camping a repel angle looking in.

reinforcements.location: Two Walls on the Customs Side facing Passport
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Small Office (Customs Desk), Right Side and Back Wall
reinforcements.note: Both reinforced. Key room to hold — has a window peek toward Waiting and a view toward Passport's reinforced walls.

reinforcements.location: Wall from Small Office toward Blue
reinforcements.note: Reinforced.

reinforcements.location: Hatch above Small Office
reinforcements.note: Reinforced — strong anchor point worth protecting.

reinforcements.location: Second Upstairs Hatch
reinforcements.note: Discrepancy: v1 leaves this open as a late drop-in point. v2 leans toward reinforcing it too unless the ELO/read calls for the drop option.

headHoles.location: Wall toward Jail from Small Office
headHoles.note: Watches anyone entering via Jail — covers the window and the door.

headHoles.location: Feet Holes facing the Supply Window
headHoles.note: No good angle from that window for a defender, and you're unlikely to be holding this room if pushed back this far — feet holes for info only.

headHoles.location: Feet Holes for the Passport Walk-In
headHoles.note: Gives info if anyone jumps in early — a common early-round move.

headHoles.location: Feet Holes watching the Supply Window Jump-In
headHoles.note: One of the best angles to stop attackers jumping this common entry window. A higher hole variant holds the window from above if upstairs is clear.

rotates.location: Rotate near Small Office, Closer to the Door
rotates.note: Saves walking all the way around. Lets you bob around and hold feet holes toward the double door.

rotates.location: Reused Top Floor Rotate Pattern (Upstairs Here)
rotates.note: Defends the upstairs portion of this site for a while, then falls back.

rotates.location: Rotate near Supply, Closer to the Door
rotates.note: Avoids crossing the exposed Supply window. Without it, the only path back is through two doors and across the window.

gadgetPlacements.note: Start at least one or two players up here early — good info room, attackers usually drone in from Break Room instead. If you do get droned, you're somewhat pinched with fewer outs.
Supply is essentially never actively held — a trap or intel gadget gets you notified the moment someone jumps the window.

operatorRecommendations.note: Not held directly — a trap or camera here buys warning time for a retake.
Good hiding/info spot since attackers rarely drone it directly — you're somewhat trapped if they do.

generalTips.note: Both sources dislike this site if given a choice — not particularly hard to defend but very easy to attack. Prioritize the other three sites first.
The entire ceiling here is soft — a ram lets attackers open a large section and plant/drop utility from directly above almost anywhere in the room.
Attacker tech: a Thermite charge on the left side of the barricade opens a Thermite-sized hole rather than the full barricade, which attackers then smoke/run through.
Suggested split: one player floating Jail/Supply early for drone kills then falling back, one or two in Customs, roughly two upstairs, one in Workshop/Vents-adjacent area.
